name: Views of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar — circular along the Ebro River
name_original: Vistas a la Basílica de Nuestra Señora del Pilar — circular por el Río Ebro
region_intro: Jogging around Ebro / Ebre offers diverse and largely flat landscapes, particularly within the Ebro Delta Natural Park. This region is characterized by extensive rice fields, numerous lagoons, and almost virgin beaches with fine sand dunes. Further upstream, the Ebro River winds through fields and mountains, providing varied terrain for running. The area's unique geography, shaped by the Ebro River, creates a dynamic backdrop for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Ebro / Ebre The most popular jogging route is Views of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar — circular along the Ebro River, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 28 minutes to complete. This easy route offers scenic views along the river. Another top favourite among local runners is Tour around the perimeter of Tudela — circular, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route provides a comprehensive loop around the town of Tudela. Local runners also love the Ebro Green Corridor Trail, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail leading through riverside woodlands, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Ebro / Ebre is defined by flat delta landscapes, riverine paths, and varied terrain further inland. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to more challenging trails with moderate elevation. The routes in Ebro / Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Ebro / Ebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Ebro / Ebre region?
A: The Ebro / Ebre region offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 50 trails available on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy riverside jogs to more challenging trails with moderate elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Ebro / Ebre?
A: The Ebro / Ebre region boasts diverse terrain. The Ebro Delta Natural Park is largely flat, featuring extensive rice fields, tranquil lagoons like Encanyissada and Tancada, and fine sand dunes near beaches such as Trabucador. Further upstream, the Ebro River winds through fields and mountains, offering varied landscapes. You can find everything from paved riverside paths to sandy trails and green corridors.

Q: Are there many circular running routes in the Ebro / Ebre area?
A: Yes, the region offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145710832/tour-around-the-perimeter-of-tudela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the perimeter of Tudela — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.1-mile (9.8 km) path. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143768260/monte-canraso-loop-from-tudela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Canraso Loop from Tudela&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) route.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see while running in the Ebro Delta?
A: While running in the Ebro Delta, you can experience unique natural features like the vast rice fields, numerous lagoons, and almost virgin beaches with fine sand dunes. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2581741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turquoise Pools and Waterfalls of Orbaneja del Castillo&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of Our Lady of the Pillar&lt;/a&gt;, which you can view on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983629485/views-of-the-basilica-of-our-lady-of-the-pilar-circular-along-the-ebro-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar — circular along the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are the running trails in Ebro / Ebre suitable for running with dogs?
A: Many trails in the Ebro / Ebre region are suitable for running with dogs, especially those through natural parks and along the river. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Ebro Delta Natural Park, to protect local wildlife. Check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Are there family-friendly running paths in the Ebro / Ebre region?
A: Yes, the Ebro / Ebre region offers several easy and flat routes perfect for families. The Ebro Delta, with its flat terrain and scenic views of rice fields and lagoons, is particularly well-suited for family runs. Look for shorter, easy-graded routes on komoot for the best family experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Ebro / Ebre?
A: The best time for running in Ebro / Ebre is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, especially in the Ebro Delta, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable then. Winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, ideal for longer runs.

Q: Can I access running routes in Ebro / Ebre using public transport?
A: Access to running routes via public transport varies across the region. Larger towns like Tudela, which features routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145679749/tudela-barranco-de-barcelosa-monte-canraso-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tudela, Barranco de Barcelosa, Monte Canraso — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, are generally well-connected by bus or train. For routes deeper within the Ebro Delta Natural Park, a car might be more convenient, though some villages within the park have limited bus services.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while running in the Ebro Delta?
A: Absolutely! The Ebro Delta is a prime location for wildlife spotting, especially birdwatching. As you run along the lagoons and through the rice fields, you might spot flamingos, herons, and many other migratory birds. The area is home to over 330 bird species, offering a unique natural immersion during your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Ebro / Ebre?
A: The Ebro / Ebre region is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse and largely flat landscapes, especially the unique environment of the Ebro Delta with its rice fields, lagoons, and serene beaches. The well-maintained paths and the opportunity for nature immersion are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in the Ebro / Ebre region?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer runs, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1140620349/ebro-green-corridor-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Green Corridor Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This trail spans 10.4 miles (16.7 km) and leads through picturesque riverside woodlands, offering a sustained running experience along the Ebro River.
tips: There is always a good atmosphere around the impressive Basilica of Nuestra Señora del Pilar. You cannot pass by this great architectural wonder located next to the Ebro River and in the historic center of Zaragoza.

Witness of the best sunsets in the city, the Puente de Piedra will take you to the Balcón de San Lázaro, where you can get one of the best photographs that will serve as a souvenir of your visit to the city.

The Santiago Bridge is a testament to the engineering and history of Zaragoza, and its construction involved challenging working conditions for the workers of the time. A landmark that connects the past and the present!
